operating_state: Revenue reached $46.7 billion in Q2, up 56% year-over-year and 6% sequentially, driven by accelerated computing platform demand for large language models and generative AI applications. Data Center revenue grew 56% annually with Blackwell architecture ramping sequentially, while compute revenue declined sequentially due to reduced H20 sales following export control restrictions. Cloud service providers represented approximately 50% of Data Center revenue, with networking revenue surging 98% annually driven by NVLink compute fabric and InfiniBand products.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed to 72.4% from 75.1% prior year as Blackwell revenue consists primarily of full-scale datacenter systems versus prior Hopper HGX systems, though improved sequentially from 60.5% following prior quarter's H20 inventory charge. Operating expenses increased 38% annually driven by compute infrastructure costs, compensation reflecting employee growth, and engineering development costs for new product introductions alongside export control compliance burdens.
material_events: Board approved additional $60 billion share repurchase authorization in August 2025 bringing total available to $71.2 billion, with $24.2 billion of shares repurchased in first half of fiscal 2026 and $488 million in dividends paid. Ellen Ochoa resigned from the Board effective July 31, 2025 for personal reasons. USG imposed new licensing requirements for H20 integrated circuits for export to China in April 2025, then granted limited licenses for certain H20 products in August 2025, though no revenue generated under those licenses to date.
macro_exposures: USG announced rescission of AI Diffusion interim final rule in May 2025 with replacement rule scope and timing uncertain, potentially imposing new product restrictions or licensing requirements. Export control restrictions have expanded significantly with worldwide licensing requirements on data center products and complex performance-based controls targeting China and other regions. Geopolitical tensions continue escalating with potential for additional controls on AI-related semiconductors, while Chinese government responses include investigations into compliance practices and potential restrictions on US semiconductor products.
forward_outlook: Management continues executing one-year product cadence for Data Center compute introductions with production shipments of GB300 Blackwell Ultra platforms beginning in Q2. Company investing in specialized U.S. manufacturing equipment and processes to support domestic production and meet growing AI infrastructure demand amid domestic manufacturing ecosystem capacity constraints. USG officials have indicated expectation of receiving 15% of revenue from licensed H20 sales, though no formal regulation has been published.
uncertainty_profile: Product transitions and sophisticated system configurations may create supply-demand management challenges resulting in revenue volatility, quality issues, or product delays. Regulatory investigations are ongoing across multiple jurisdictions including French Competition Authority inquiries and broad information requests from competition regulators globally. The company faces potential financial penalties and operational restrictions from Chinese regulators investigating compliance with export controls, while future export control changes could subject wider product ranges to restrictions and licensing requirements.
